2606.07631.title: Trait-space Monitoring for Emergent Misalignment During Supervised Finetuning
2606.07631.paper_summary: Emergent misalignment (EM) occurs when narrow finetuning causes a model to behave dangerously outside the finetuning task. Standard training signals can miss this shift, making reliable detection costly if it depends on repeated behavioral evaluation. We ask whether emergent misalignment can instead be detected from internal representations during finetuning. Using seven alignment-relevant traits encoded as linear directions in activation space, we track representational drift across training checkpoints in four open-source 7-9B LLMs. EM-relevant drift concentrates on a low-dimensional axis that explains 65.5% of the variance, revealing a geometric signature in the studied regime. A low-overhead monitor built on this drift profile detects dangerous checkpoints with 2.2% false negative rate, 2.9% false positive rate, and 0.990 AUROC on held-out perturbation types, outperforming unsupervised PCA and SAE baselines. Stress tests on two 14B models, longer finetuning runs, and misaligned starting points identify key deployment boundaries. These results position trait-space monitoring as a practical complement to behavioral evaluation for EM detection during LoRA-based finetuning, while showing that deployment across substantially different regimes may require recalibration.
2606.07631.paper_summary_zh: 新興不對齊（EM）發生在狹義微調導致模型在微調任務之外的行為危險時。標準訓練信號可能會錯過這一變化，如果依賴於重複的行為評估，則可靠檢測的成本會很高。我們詢問是否可以從微調過程中的內部表示來檢測新興不對齊。利用七個與對齊相關的特徵，這些特徵在激活空間中編碼為線性方向，我們追踪四個開源7-9B LLMs在訓練檢查點之間的表示漂移。與EM相關的漂移集中在一個低維軸上，解釋了65.5%的方差，揭示了所研究範疇中的幾何特徵。基於這一漂移特徵構建的低開銷監控器以2.2%的假陰性率、2.9%的假陽性率以及0.990的AUROC在保留的擾動類型上檢測危險檢查點，超越了無監督PCA和SAE基準。在兩個14B模型上的壓力測試、更長的微調運行和不對齊的起始點識別了關鍵的部署邊界。這些結果將特徵空間監控定位為在基於LoRA的微調過程中檢測EM的實用補充，同時顯示在顯著不同的範疇之間的部署可能需要重新校準。
2606.07631.author: Huy Nghiem et.al.
2606.07631.authors: Huy Nghiem, Sy-Tuyen Ho, Sarah Wiegreffe, Hal Daumé
